In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

deleting columns that aren't needed to the question¶

In [8]:
# drop all columns containing 'think_time' in their name
cols = [c for c in df.columns if 'think_time' in c]
df.drop(columns=cols, inplace=True)
df.head()

Make a visualization to understand the data correlation¶

In [11]:
sns.heatmap(df.corr(), annot=True, cmap="coolwarm")
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap")
Out[11]:
Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Correlation Heatmap')
seeing how deleting rows of zeros changes the data¶
In [12]:
 
df = df[(df != 0).all(axis=1)]
df.head()

In [13]:
### making a scatter plot to visualize the relationship between 'social_media_usage' and 'stress_level'
sns.scatterplot(data=df, x='time_loss_social_media', y='nervous_anxious')
plt.title('Scatter Plot of Social Media Usage vs Stress Level')
plt.xlabel('Social Media Usage (hours per day)')
Out[13]:
Text(0.5, 0, 'Social Media Usage (hours per day)')
In [14]:
sns.scatterplot(
    data=df,
    x="social_media_before_bed",
    y="mood_disorder",
    size="time_loss_social_media",
    legend=False,
    sizes=(20, 200)
)
plt.title("Social Media Before Bed vs Mood Disorder")
plt.xlabel("Social Media Before Bed")
plt.ylabel("Mood Disorder")
plt.show()
In [15]:
ordered_levels_df = df['social_media_before_bed'].value_counts().index
g_df = sns.FacetGrid(df, row='social_media_before_bed', row_order=ordered_levels_df, height=1.7, aspect=4)
g_df.map(sns.kdeplot, 'time_loss_social_media')
plt.xlabel('Time Loss Social Media')
plt.show()
In [16]:
# FacetGrids of mood_disorder by actual time spent on each app
actual_apps = [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith("actual_time_")]

for col in actual_apps:
    ordered_levels = sorted(df[col].dropna().unique())
    g = sns.FacetGrid(df, row=col, row_order=ordered_levels, height=1.6, aspect=4)
    g.map(sns.kdeplot, "mood_disorder", fill=True)
    g.set_xlabels("Mood Disorder")
    g.fig.suptitle(f"Mood Disorder by {col.replace('actual_time_','').capitalize()} usage", y=1.02)
    plt.show()

c:\Users\parni\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\seaborn\axisgrid.py:854: UserWarning: Dataset has 0 variance; skipping density estimate. Pass `warn_singular=False` to disable this warning.
  func(*plot_args, **plot_kwargs)
In [17]:
# create a single FacetGrid for all apps showing KDEs of mood_disorder by usage level
from sqlalchemy import func


melted = df.melt(id_vars=['mood_disorder'], value_vars=actual_apps, var_name='app', value_name='usage')
melted['app'] = melted['app'].str.replace('actual_time_', '').str.capitalize()
melted['usage'] = melted['usage'].astype(str)

row_order = [c.replace('actual_time_', '').capitalize() for c in actual_apps]

g = sns.FacetGrid(melted, row='app', row_order=row_order, hue='usage', height=1.6, aspect=4, palette='tab10')
g.map(sns.kdeplot, 'mood_disorder', common_norm=False, fill=True, alpha=0.6)
g.set_xlabels('Mood Disorder')
g.add_legend(title='Usage')
g.fig.suptitle('Mood Disorder by App Usage', y=1.02)
warn_singular=False

plt.show()

c:\Users\parni\anaconda3\Lib\site-packages\seaborn\axisgrid.py:854: UserWarning: Dataset has 0 variance; skipping density estimate. Pass `warn_singular=False` to disable this warning.
  func(*plot_args, **plot_kwargs)
